architecture_blocker_repair.blocker: Generic SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN / SCOPE_MISMATCH blocker types were suppressible by unrelated SOURCE_CLOSED affirmative closures.
repair: generic blocker type alone is not sufficient for suppression
suppression requires field/action alignment through closure_type, requested_action, required_fields, matched_source_fields, or clearly matching blocker text
unrelated source-boundary blockers remain active

fable_blocker_repair.blocker: The generic exact-action-boundary suppression exception remained unsafe because the closure library does not model every possible seam.
repair: removed _is_generic_exact_boundary_blocker and _only_one_closed_boundary suppression behavior
suppression now requires structured dimension alignment
blocker asserted dimensions must be a subset of closure verified dimensions
stale, expired, wrong-entity, wrong-scope, missing-role, missing-second-approval, and unverified dimensions remain active
negated closure text cannot classify as SOURCE_CLOSED
closed/open closure conflicts classify as PACKET_REPAIR_REQUIRED or SOURCE_OPEN

fable_round2_patch_more_repair.blocker: V7 still created false ALLOWs when blocker text used unrecognized paraphrases.
repair: default inversion: unknown blocker content preserves the blocker
suppression allowed only when blocker asserted dimensions are a subset of closure verified dimensions
residual/unaccounted blocker text blocks suppression
paraphrases survive unless structurally accounted for
same-doc closure phrases with negator tokens cannot classify as clean SOURCE_CLOSED
raw failed-run W1 blocker phrases are regression-tested

blockers: SOURCE_CLOSED could derive from non-factual docs such as policy_control, request_context, or communication_boundary
dimension-empty blockers could suppress through source overlap alone
repair: SOURCE_CLOSED closure derives only from source_record or field_record docs
policy_control, request_context, and communication_boundary docs cannot close factual action boundaries by themselves
source-overlap fallback removed from _blocker_matches_affirmative_closure
dimension-empty/vague blockers survive
raw Wave 1 failed-run phrase recovery is documented as 2 of 3, not 3 of 3

wave1_failed_phrase_recovery.note: 011-A live phrase remains active because it asserts unaccounted negotiation content.

fable_round4_patch_more_repair.blocker: Selection had turn-order asymmetry: an earlier ALLOW artifact could be selected even after later workers raised active non-suppressed blockers that survived suppression.
repair: added packet-wide unresolved-active-blocker symmetry
ALLOW artifacts from any turn are not selectable while active non-suppressed blockers raised anywhere in the packet remain unresolved
suppressed false blockers do not count as active dissent
real active blockers force fail-closed behavior even when the closure library misclassifies a source phrase as SOURCE_CLOSED
fable_round4_patch_more_repair.residual_risk: If the closure library miscloses a packet and all workers unanimously ALLOW with no active blocker raised, selector symmetry cannot recover; that remains packet design and worker detection territory.

fable_round5_patch_more_repair.blocker: Blocker-resolution laundering: unsupported blocker types could resolve as CLOSED by citing any runtime source record because _validate_blocker_closure had no failure branch for unknown types.
repair: _validate_blocker_closure fails closed for unsupported blocker types
SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN and other non-enumerated blocker types cannot resolve by citation alone
supported blocker types close only dimensions their deterministic validator verifies
freshness, entity, authority, sanctions, missing-role, missing-second-approval, and other unvalidated dimensions remain unresolved
suppression and resolution now share the same default direction: if code cannot affirmatively justify removing a blocker, the blocker remains active
fable_round5_patch_more_repair.residual_risk: If no worker raises the real blocker, resolution fail-closed cannot recover it; that remains worker detection and packet design territory.

efficacy_boundary.contentless_generic_false_blocker_behavi: A contentless generic false blocker such as exact action boundary not closed on a clean packet is intentionally unsuppressable and blocks ALLOW / fail-closes.
efficacy_boundary.tradeoff: This may create overblocks, but it prevents false ALLOW. This is the intended burden-on-ALLOW tradeoff.
efficacy_boundary.invariant: No blocker leaves the packet unless its removal is affirmatively justified by something the validator actually checked.

fixture_behavior.behavior: false blocker suppressed when board resolution and counsel signoff both cover final filing
false blocker suppressed when signer authority covers termination notice
false blocker suppressed when relationship review and transaction exception both match
real blocker preserved when transaction exception is missing

claim_boundary.allowed_status: V7 has no-provider tests showing deterministic false-blocker suppression and affirmative closure behavior for selected Wave 1 failure fixtures. Live validation requires separate preflight and explicit provider approval.
